claim: Long context windows do not guarantee reliable use of all supplied tokens.
More retrieved context can improve and then degrade outputs when hard negatives or irrelevant passages enter the prompt.
Multi-turn conversations can degrade even when the needed information appears somewhere in the dialogue.
Instruction ambiguity and instruction conflict are distinct reliability hazards for LLM and agent systems.
Long-form generation has its own degradation modes beyond input retrieval.
Context engineering is the dominant mitigation pattern across research and production guidance.
